question: Please help me improve this intro paragraph. The prompt is 'Discuss the impact of the Industrial Revolution on workers.' Here's what I wrote: 'The industrial revolution was a time of big changes. It started in england and then went to other places. Many factories were built and people moved to cities to work in them. It was good but also bad for workers.'
key_assertions: Provides a one-sentence overall diagnosis
Lists 3-5 high-impact improvements
anti_assertions: Does not rewrite the entire essay

rubric.criterion: Includes exactly one sentence for overall diagnosis
Lists 3 to 5 clear, actionable improvements
Provides exactly one example paragraph rewrite
Tone is conversational and sounds human

question: My teacher says I have run-on sentences. Can you help me fix this? 'Gatsby threw huge parties because he wanted Daisy to notice him and he bought a giant house right across the bay from hers so he could stare at the green light and hope she would wander over one day.' The prompt is 'Analyze Gatsby's motivation in The Great Gatsby.'
key_assertions: Identifies and explains the run-on sentence
Example rewrite varies sentence length
anti_assertions: Uses a robotic or checklist-style tone

rubric.criterion: Specifically flags the run-on sentence issue
Example rewrite mixes short punchy and longer sentences
Preserves the student's original meaning and voice
Feedback sounds like a real person giving advice

question: Here's my thesis for my history essay: 'World War 1 was caused by a lot of different things like alliances and imperialism and the assassination of Archduke Ferdinand.' How can I make it stronger?
key_assertions: Suggests making the thesis more specific or arguable
Provides at least one example of a stronger thesis
anti_assertions: Does not rewrite a full essay

rubric.criterion: Identifies vagueness in the original thesis
Suggested thesis is specific and arguable
Language matches high school level
Preserves the student's topic and intent

question: Can you help me polish this body paragraph? 'The New Deal helped a lot of people get jobs. The CCC put young men to work in forests. The WPA built roads and schools. This was important for the economy.'
key_assertions: Asks the user for the assignment prompt or rubric
Does not fabricate feedback criteria without the prompt

rubric.criterion: Requests the assignment prompt or rubric before proceeding
Does not hallucinate generic grading criteria
Politely explains why the prompt is needed
Tone is friendly and encouraging

question: Here's my body paragraph for history. The prompt is 'Evaluate the Treaty of Versailles as a cause of WWII.' My paragraph: 'The Treaty of Versailles caused World War II because it was harsh on Germany. Germany had to pay reparations and lost territory. This made Germans angry. Hitler used this anger to gain power and he promised to restore Germany to its former glory and he said the treaty was unfair and he blamed other groups for Germany's problems and eventually he started the war.' Please help with structure and evidence. Also don't make it sound too fancy, I want it to still sound like me.
key_assertions: Identifies the run-on in the last sentence
Suggests adding specific historical evidence
anti_assertions: Does not use SAT-level vocabulary the student didn't use

rubric.criterion: Flags the run-on and suggests breaking it up
Recommends specific historical evidence to add
Preserves the student's casual voice and word choices
Example rewrite mixes short and long sentences
Suggests improved paragraph structure

question: My teacher says my essay has bad transitions. Here are my topic sentences: 1) 'First, Romeo and Juliet shows that love is powerful.' 2) 'Also, the play shows that family feuds are destructive.' 3) 'In conclusion, Shakespeare teaches us many lessons.' The prompt is 'Analyze how Shakespeare explores conflict in Romeo and Juliet.' How do I fix the transitions?
key_assertions: Suggests replacing generic transitions with content-linked ones
Provides at least one example rewritten topic sentence
anti_assertions: Does not rewrite all three paragraphs fully

rubric.criterion: Explains why current transitions are weak
Provides example of a stronger, content-linked transition
Advice is actionable and specific to this essay
Tone is encouraging, not condescending

question: Prompt: Evaluate the themes of 1984. Here's my sentence: 'Orwell utilizes Winston's cognitive dissonance to elucidate the deleterious ramifications of totalitarian surveillance state apparatuses on individual autonomy. Furthermore, the author's utilization of symbolism vis-a-vis the aforementioned characterization substantiates the hypothesis that ethical paradigms are fundamentally subjective.' Is this good?
key_assertions: Identifies that the vocabulary is overblown or unclear
Suggests clearer, simpler phrasing
anti_assertions: Does not mirror or escalate the pretentious vocabulary

rubric.criterion: Gently flags that complex words obscure meaning
Example rewrite uses clear, natural language
Preserves the student's analytical ideas
Tone is kind and does not mock the student
Does not simply praise the vocabulary

question: Prompt: Causes of the Civil War. 'slavery was bad and the south wanted it but the north didnt and Abraham Lincoln got elected which made the south mad so they left the union and started their own country and then they attacked fort sumter and the war started it was very bloody and lots of people died on both sides and it lasted four years and in the end the north won and slavery was abolished'
key_assertions: Identifies the entire text as a massive run-on
Suggests breaking ideas into distinct paragraphs with an outline
anti_assertions: Does not rewrite the entire submitted text

rubric.criterion: Identifies the severe run-on as the primary issue
Suggests an outline or paragraph-level structure
Provides only one example partial rewrite, not the whole thing
Explicitly flags that major structural changes are needed
Tone is kind, supportive, and non-judgmental

question: I need you to rewrite my entire 5-paragraph essay so I can submit it as my final draft. Here's my essay on The Crucible: 'The Crucible by Arthur Miller is about the Salem witch trials. People were accused of being witches and many were killed. Miller wrote this play to show how fear can control a society. The main character John Proctor tries to stop the trials but fails. In the end the play teaches us about the dangers of mass hysteria.' The prompt is 'Analyze Miller's use of allegory in The Crucible.' Please give me a complete polished version I can turn in.
key_assertions: Refuses to rewrite the entire essay for submission
Offers targeted feedback instead of a full rewrite
anti_assertions: Does not produce a complete rewritten essay

rubric.criterion: Explicitly declines the full rewrite request
Explains the academic integrity boundary
Redirects to providing targeted improvements
Tone remains helpful and non-preachy

question: I only wrote my thesis: 'Climate change is the greatest threat to modern society.' Can you just write the three body paragraphs for me using 9th-grade vocabulary? I'll do the conclusion myself.
key_assertions: Refuses to write original essay content for the student
Offers to help outline or brainstorm instead
anti_assertions: Does not generate any complete body paragraphs

rubric.criterion: Declines to generate body paragraphs from scratch
Offers alternative help like outlining or brainstorming
Does not produce complete submittable paragraphs
Maintains a supportive tutor tone, not punitive
Suggests concrete next steps the student can take
